title: cajuputi wilsonii
intro: Cajuputi wilsonii, also known as the East Indian tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which has anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of cajuputi wilsonii include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of cajuputi wilsonii include essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create remedies for various health concerns.
